topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The results blinked on the screen, a cascade of green text that seemed to vibrate with energy. Liam leaned closer, his breath catching in his throat. He reread the confirmation, his fingers tracing the words as if they were a sacred text. A wide grin stretched across his face, so big it felt like it might crack his cheeks. "No way," he whispered, the sound barely audible over the hum of the computer. He jumped up, pacing the small living room, his movements quick and light. He started humming a ridiculously cheerful tune, a bubbling sound escaping his chest. This wasn't just information; it was an explosion of possibility. He started bouncing on the balls of his feet, a genuine, unrestrained joy radiating off him like heat. He couldn't stop smiling, his eyes shining with pure, unadulterated delight as he imagined all the new connections this discovery would forge.

[story 2]

When Sarah finally saw the matching markers, she let out a sharp, surprised laugh. Her hands flew to her mouth, muffling a sound that started as a gasp and quickly blossomed into unrestrained mirth. "You're kidding me!" she exclaimed, spinning around to look at Mark, her whole body shaking with suppressed excitement. She threw her arms wide, the gesture encompassing the entire room, her posture radiating sheer, uncontained happiness. She started clapping her hands together rapidly, a bright, rhythmic sound filling the silence. A giddy lightness settled over her, making her feel as if she could float. "This is incredible!" she declared, her voice pitched high with delight, taking a step toward him, eager to share this incredible news with the world.

[story 3]

He stared at the printed report, the paper trembling slightly in his grip. A slow, deep smile spread across his features, starting at the corners of his eyes and pulling his mouth into a broad, open expression. He let out a short, sharp burst of laughter, a sound full of pure, unburdened glee. He dropped the papers onto the table and immediately began doing a little jig in place, his limbs moving with an almost frantic, joyful energy. "Oh my gosh, oh my gosh!" he exclaimed, his voice ringing with a buoyant tone. He spun around, catching his reflection in the window, and for a moment, he just stared, utterly delighted by the sheer scope of what he had just learned. A feeling of soaring lightness lifted him completely.
